text.source_text: अत्रोच्यते — न तथादृष्ट्युपदेशादिति । न शब्दादिभ्यः कारणेभ्यः परमेश्वरस्य प्रत्याख्यानं युक्तम् । कुतः ? तथा जाठरापरित्यागेन , दृष्ट्युपदेशात् । परमेश्वरदृष्टिर्हि जाठरे वैश्वानरे इहोपदिश्यते — ‘ मनो ब्रह्मेत्युपासीत ’ (छा. उ. ३ । १८ । १) इत्यादिवत् । अथवा जाठर वैश्वानरोपाधिः परमेश्वर इह द्रष्टव्यत्वेनोपदिश्यते — ‘ मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः ’ (छा. उ. ३ । १४ । २) इत्यादिवत् । यदि चे ह परमेश्वरो न विवक्ष्येत , केवल एव जाठरोऽग्निर्विवक्ष्येत , ततः ‘ मूर्धैव सुतेजाः ’ इत्यादेर्विशेषस्यासम्भव एव स्यात् । यथा तु देवताभूताग्निव्यपाश्रयेणाप्ययं विशेष उपपादयितुं न शक्यते , तथोत्तरसूत्रे वक्ष्यामः । यदि च केवल एव जाठरो विवक्ष्येत , पुरुषेऽन्तःप्रतिष्ठितत्वं केवलं तस्य स्यात् । न तु पुरुषत्वम् । पुरुषमपि चैनमधीयते वाजसनेयिनः — ‘ स एषोऽग्निर्वैश्वानरो यत्पुरुषः स यो हैतमेवमग्निं वैश्वानरं पुरुषं पुरुषेऽन्तः प्रतिष्ठितं वेद ’ (श. ब्रा. १० । ६ । १ । ११) इति । परमेश्वरस्य तु सर्वात्मत्वात्पुरुषत्वं पुरुषेऽन्तःप्रतिष्ठितत्वं चोभयमुपपद्यते । ये तु ‘ पुरुषविधमपि चैनमधीयते ’ इति सूत्रावयवं पठन्ति , तेषामेषोऽर्थः — केवलजाठरपरिग्रहे पुरुषेऽन्तःप्रतिष्ठितत्वं केवलं स्यात् । न तु पुरुषविधत्वम् । पुरुषविधमपि चैनमधीयते वाजसनेयिनः — ‘ पुरुषविधं पुरुषेऽन्तः प्रतिष्ठितं वेद ’ इति । पुरुषविधत्वं च प्रकरणात् यदधिदैवतं द्युमूर्धत्वादि पृथिवीप्रतिष्ठितत्वान्तम् , यच्चाध्यात्मं प्रसिद्धं मूर्धत्वादि चुबुकप्रतिष्ठितत्वान्तम् , तत्परिगृह्यते ॥ २६ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: atrocyate — na tathādṛṣṭyupadeśāditi | na śabdādibhyaḥ kāraṇebhyaḥ parameśvarasya pratyākhyānaṃ yuktam | kutaḥ ? tathā jāṭharāparityāgena , dṛṣṭyupadeśāt | parameśvaradṛṣṭirhi jāṭhare vaiśvānare ihopadiśyate — ‘ mano brahmetyupāsīta ’ (chā. u. 3 | 18 | 1) ityādivat | athavā jāṭhara vaiśvānaropādhiḥ parameśvara iha draṣṭavyatvenopadiśyate — ‘ manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ ’ (chā. u. 3 | 14 | 2) ityādivat | yadi ce ha parameśvaro na vivakṣyeta , kevala eva jāṭharo'gnirvivakṣyeta , tataḥ ‘ mūrdhaiva sutejāḥ ’ ityāderviśeṣasyāsambhava eva syāt | yathā tu devatābhūtāgnivyapāśrayeṇāpyayaṃ viśeṣa upapādayituṃ na śakyate , tathottarasūtre vakṣyāmaḥ | yadi ca kevala eva jāṭharo vivakṣyeta , puruṣe'ntaḥpratiṣṭhitatvaṃ kevalaṃ tasya syāt | na tu puruṣatvam | puruṣamapi cainamadhīyate vājasaneyinaḥ — ‘ sa eṣo'gnirvaiśvānaro yatpuruṣaḥ sa yo haitamevamagniṃ vaiśvānaraṃ puruṣaṃ puruṣe'ntaḥ pratiṣṭhitaṃ veda ’ (śa. brā. 10 | 6 | 1 | 11) iti | parameśvarasya tu sarvātmatvātpuruṣatvaṃ puruṣe'ntaḥpratiṣṭhitatvaṃ cobhayamupapadyate | ye tu ‘ puruṣavidhamapi cainamadhīyate ’ iti sūtrāvayavaṃ paṭhanti , teṣāmeṣo'rthaḥ — kevalajāṭharaparigrahe puruṣe'ntaḥpratiṣṭhitatvaṃ kevalaṃ syāt | na tu puruṣavidhatvam | puruṣavidhamapi cainamadhīyate vājasaneyinaḥ — ‘ puruṣavidhaṃ puruṣe'ntaḥ pratiṣṭhitaṃ veda ’ iti | puruṣavidhatvaṃ ca prakaraṇāt yadadhidaivataṃ dyumūrdhatvādi pṛthivīpratiṣṭhitatvāntam , yaccādhyātmaṃ prasiddhaṃ mūrdhatvādi cubukapratiṣṭhitatvāntam , tatparigṛhyate || 26 ||

translation.literal_translation: Reply: not so, because of teaching of contemplative vision. Rejection of the Supreme based on word and related reasons is not justified. Why? Because the instruction teaches a vision in that manner - without abandoning digestive fire as basis. Here a contemplation of the Supreme in digestive Vaishvanara is taught, as with 'one should meditate on mind as Brahman.' Or, the Supreme qualified by digestive-fire adjunct is taught as object of realization, as with 'mind-made, life-bodied, radiant.' If the Supreme were not intended and only digestive fire intended, then specific marks like 'heaven is head' would be impossible. How this specification cannot be adequately established even by appeal to deity or elemental fire will be shown in the next sutra. Again, if only digestive fire were intended, one might have only 'established within the person,' but not true personhood. Yet the Vajasaneyins also read this as person: 'this is that Vaishvanara fire who is person; whoever knows this Vaishvanara fire as person established within person...' For the Supreme, however, both personhood and inner-establishment are fitting, because He is self of all. Those who read the sutra-member as 'and they also read him as person-like' take the point thus: on a merely digestive reading, inner-establishment alone would fit, not person-form. But Vajasaneyins read even person-form established in person. And this person-form is interpreted by taking both the macrocosmic (heaven-head ... earth-base) and microcosmic (known bodily head ... chin-base) correspondences from context. (26)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.26 resolves objection by drsti-upadesa: jatharagni serves as upadhi/alambana for Brahman-contemplation; textual personhood and cosmic correspondence exceed literal digestive-fire scope.
translation.interpretive_note: Meditative superimposition structure preserves ritual surface while fixing metaphysical referent as Brahman.
